_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Clinical presentations consistent with Liver Abscess (e.g., fever, chills, abdominal pain restricted to the right upper quadrant, hepatic tenderness)

Laboratory findings indicative of Liver Abscess (e.g., anemia, leukocytosis, high erythrocyte sedimentation rate, elevated C-reactive protein level, hypoalbuminemia, hyperbilirubinemia, elevated alanine aminotransferase (ALAT) and alkaline phosphatase (AP) levels)
Imaging (Ultrasound or Computed Tomography) confirming the presence of Liver Abscess
Positive amebic serology for amebic Liver Abscess or microbiological findings for pyogenic Liver Abscess

preauth.label: Clinical Notes including evaluation findings, indications for the procedure, and planned line of treatment

claim.label: Detailed Indoor Case Papers with treatment/aspiration details

ppd_preauth.q: Was the Clinical Notes including evaluation findings, indications for the procedure, and planned line of treatment submitted?
Was the liver function test report submitted?
Did the pretreatment USG Abdomen submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Was detailed Indoor Case Papers with vital (BP and Pulse) and treatment/fluid aspiration details submitted?
Was the post treatment USG Abdomen report of patient submitted?
Was the detailed discharge Summary submitted with the date of the follow-up mentioned?

common_queries: Clinical notes and/or USG Abdomen report not indicative of liver abscess
Liver Function Test report missing during pre-authorization
Pretreatment USG Abdomen report missing or unclear at pre-authorization
Detailed Indoor Case Papers missing or incomplete at claim submission
Post-treatment USG Abdomen report missing at claim submission

additional_information.min_doctor_qualification_desirable: DNB / MD (General Medicine / Pediatric Medicine/Radio diagnosis); MS General surgery.
clinical_key_pointers: Liver abscess (LA) is an uncommon but potentially life-threatening disease with significant morbidity and mortality.
The majority of these abscesses are categorized into pyogenic or amoebic, although a minority is caused by parasites and fungi.
Most amoebic infections are caused by Entamoeba histolytica.
The pyogenic abscesses are usually polymicrobial, but some organisms are seen more commonly in them, such as E.coli, Klebsiella, Streptococcus, Staphylococcus, and anaerobes.
surgical_options: Abscess puncture and drainage (implied for pyogenic LA)
additional_information.post_op_care: Follow-up advice mentioned in the detailed discharge summary.
